source_notes.official_book: NBA official scorer report uploaded by user: 20260505_CLEDET_book (2).pdf
source_notes.recap: AP recap supplied by user: Cade Cunningham scores 23, Tobias Harris has 20 to help Pistons beat Cavs 111-101 in Game 1.

turning_points: Detroit opened the game with a 37-21 first quarter, forcing Cleveland into 6 first-quarter turnovers that produced 12 Pistons points.
Cleveland entered halftime down 59-46, creating a live team-total window where Cleveland needed 55 points to clear 100.5.
Cleveland produced a 30-point third quarter on 72.2% shooting and 66.7% from three, immediately validating the second-half offensive recovery path.
Cleveland tied the game 93-93 with 5:28 left after James Harden scored seven straight, but Detroit answered through Jalen Duren rim finishes off Cade Cunningham creation.
Cleveland landed exactly 101 points, clearing the 100.5 team total by a half-point hook.

Cade Cunningham.model_relevance: Late-game creation stabilized Detroit; assisted three straight Jalen Duren dunks after Cleveland tied the game.

Tobias Harris.model_relevance: Two-way veteran profile helped Detroit survive Cleveland second-half push.

Jalen Duren.model_relevance: Late rim-finishing and defensive stop on Harden were pivotal; rebound funnel validated.

Duncan Robinson.model_relevance: Spacing and shot-making punished Cleveland defensive rotations.

Donovan Mitchell.model_relevance: Scoring was adequate but did not reach opener ceiling profile; streak of 30+ in series openers ended.

James Harden.model_relevance: Fourth-quarter scoring surge carried Cleveland TT over, but turnovers were the core game-loss driver.

Jarrett Allen.model_relevance: Center displacement/foul-limited profile created rotation instability; Cleveland interior scoring/rebound ceiling reduced.

live_entry_context.edge_quality: Playable thin edge; dependent on Cleveland reducing turnovers and maintaining 3PT volume.

success_drivers: Cleveland shot 51.2% in the second half and scored exactly 55 after halftime.
Third-quarter correction was the key: 30 points on 13-18 FG and 4-6 3PT.
Harden's 13 fourth-quarter points generated enough late scoring pressure to reach 101.
Detroit's lead size kept Cleveland starters active deep into the fourth quarter, protecting minutes and usage.
risk_notes: Cleveland committed 20 total turnovers, giving Detroit 31 points off turnovers.
Jarrett Allen was limited to 18:22 with 4 fouls and only 2 points / 3 rebounds.
The edge was real but fragile: no cushion beyond the hook.
patch_recommendations: Tag HALFTIME_TT_OVER_HOOK_WIN when a team total clears by 0.5 with exact second-half requirement met.
Do not over-grade as high-confidence despite win; classify as THIN_EDGE_CONFIRMED because the final landed exactly on the needed second-half number.
Add Cleveland turnover dampener when Harden/Mitchell dual-handler lineups face aggressive point-of-attack pressure.
Keep Detroit live spread/moneyline resilience boost active when Duren + Cade PNR is functioning late.

betting_result_summary.notes: LiveFlow halftime Cleveland TT over 100.5 cashed by the hook. Correct read on second-half correction, but the narrow margin requires thin-edge classification rather than full model dominance.
